report.title: `endpoint!` / `outpoint!` URL literal check rejects every `<**path>` catch-all
report.summary: The compile-time URL format check in `hotaru_trans` calls `parse_check_url` with the source representation of the string literal (quotes included) instead of its unescaped value, so the trailing `"` lands in the final segment and the parser reports `AnyPathMixedWithOtherContent`. Every `<**path>` usage in `endpoint!`, `outpoint!`, and their semi-trans / attr forms is rejected at macro expansion time even though the underlying runtime parser would accept it.

impact: Blocks any route that uses a multi-segment catch-all. `APP.url("/static/<**path>")`, `#[url("/files/<**path:rest>")]`, and `#[endpoint("/static/<**path>")]` all fail to compile with `Invalid URL literal format: <**path> must be the only content within its segment at index N`. No workaround within the macro — users have to comment out the route and rebuild it as multiple `<str:…>` levels, which is not equivalent because single-segment patterns will not match `/static/css/main.css`.

behavior_diff.expected: Macro-time URL format check sees the same string the runtime sees (e.g. `/static/<**path>`, 16 chars), and the catch-all is accepted as the sole content of its segment.
behavior_diff.actual: `Literal::to_string()` returns the source representation (`"/static/<**path>"`, 18 chars including the quotes). The final segment is parsed as `<**path>"`, which fails the "AnyPath must be the only content of its segment" check at `hotaru_core/src/url/parser.rs`.

status.fix: `hotaru_trans/src/url/urlexpr.rs` — `check_url_literal_format` now passes the unescaped value to `parse_check_url`. Added a `strip_str_literal_quotes` helper that peels `"…"`, `r"…"`, and `r#"…"#` (any hash count) before parsing.
verification: Downstream sandbox: `endpoint! { APP.url("/static/<**path>"), … }` compiles and `curl /static/css/main.css` + `curl /static/deep/nested/file.txt` both route to the handler with HTTP 200
